title: Monsoon Moist Peruvian Egg Omelette
description: A savory and velvety Peruvian dish featuring free-range eggs, score-fat, and jain-friendly ingredients, perfect for a monsoon season breakfast. The combination of eggs, onions, and aji amarillo peppers creates a delicious and spicy flavor profile.
instructions: Heat the melted score-fat in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they are translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the chopped aji amarillo peppers and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Pour the beaten eggs over the onion and pepper mixture in the skillet.
Cook the eggs until the edges start to set, then use a spatula to gently lift and fold the edges of the omelette towards the center.
Continue cooking the eggs until they are almost set, then use the spatula to fold the omelette in half.
Cook for an additional 30 seconds to 1 minute, until the eggs are fully set and the omelette is cooked through.
Season the omelette with salt and black pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve hot.
